title: Burette (Fluid Administration)
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- Administration of a specific volume of intravenous fluids/medications, especially with paediatrics. 

#### Procedure

- Remove fluid from outer bag and check for discolouration or sedimentation.

- Confirm “5 rights” with paramedic partner, **the right**: 
- Patient
- Drug
- Dose
- Time
- Route
- Remove burette from pack and turn off the drop regulator above fluid chamber.

- Do not turn off air slide.

- Reposition drip regulator near fluid chamber and turn off the roller regulator.

- Connect a giving set to the port below the burette’s fluid chamber.

- Remove protective cap from both fluid bag and burette perforator, taking care not to contaminate either. Discard should contamination occur.

- Insert perforator into fluid bag port taking care not to puncture the port wall.

- Open the burette’s roller regulator and allow 100mls to fill fluid chamber. Open the giving set’s roller regulator and prime the line. Squeeze the lower chamber several times until half full. Ensure all air is removed from the line. Close the regulator.

- Reopen the burette’s regulator and refill the chamber to 100mls.

- Attach the giving set line to the patient’s cannula and secure.

#### Specific Notes

- Refill chamber as required.
- Control flow with regulator on giving set.
- Always ensure medication pre administration checks are completed prior to any drug administration.
